etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "hanker" + "-ing" from Dutch "hunkeren" (to long for)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: Longing for; having longing craving and hankering longing with craving presence
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Longing for → longing craving → contemporary hankering longing and hankering craving with longing presence
etymology_and_history.cultural_shifts: Shifts**: Longing for → longing craving → hankering longing → longing understanding

contemporary_usage.primary_definition: Definition**: Having hankering longing and hankering craving; experiencing longing craving and hankering hankeringness with hankering presence
contemporary_usage.colloquial_usage: Usage**: "Hankering for food," "hankering after adventure," "strong hankering"
contemporary_usage.register: Positive mild; indicates longing and craving desire with yearning longing
